title: Distributive property
description: Practice decomposing the factors in multiplication problems and see how it affects the product.

hints: Let's use a tape diagram to break the $8\times7$ into two parts.

The tape diagram breaks $8\times7$  into $\purpleE{5\times7}$ and $\maroonE{3\times7}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
{8} \times {7} &=(\purpleE{5\times7}) + (\maroonE{3\times7})\\\\
&=\purpleE{35}+\maroonE{21}\\\\
&=56
\end{align}$
Let's use a tape diagram to break the $8\times6$ into two parts.

The tape diagram breaks $8\times6$  into $\purpleE{4\times6}$ and $\maroonE{4\times6}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
{8} \times {6} &=(\purpleE{4\times6}) + (\maroonE{4\times6})\\\\
&=\purpleE{24}+\maroonE{24}\\\\
&=48
\end{align}$
Let's use a tape diagram to break the $8\times9$ into two parts.

The tape diagram breaks $8\times9$  into $\purpleE{5\times9}$ and $\maroonE{3\times9}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
{8} \times {9} &=(\purpleE{5\times9}) + (\maroonE{3\times9})\\\\
&=\purpleE{45}+\maroonE{27}\\\\
&=72
\end{align}$
Let's use an array to break the $9\times8$ into two parts.

The array breaks $9\times8$  into $\purpleE{10\times8}$ and $\maroonE{1\times8}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
{9} \times {8} &=(\purpleE{10\times8}) - (\maroonE{1\times8})\\\\
&=\purpleE{80}-\maroonE{8}\\\\
&=72
\end{align}$
Let's use an array to break the $9\times7$ into two parts.

The array breaks $9\times7$  into $\purpleE{10\times7}$ and $\maroonE{1\times7}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 2]]

$\begin{align}
{9} \times {7} &=(\purpleE{10\times7}) - (\maroonE{1\times7})\\\\
&=\purpleE{70}-\maroonE{7}\\\\
&=63
\end{align}$
Let's use a tape diagram to break the $7\times7$ into two parts.

The tape diagram breaks $7\times7$  into $\purpleE{5\times7}$ and $\maroonE{2\times7}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
{7} \times {7} &=(\purpleE{5\times7}) + (\maroonE{2\times7})\\\\
&=\purpleE{35}+\maroonE{14}\\\\
&=49
\end{align}$
Let's use a tape diagram to break the $7\times8$ into two parts.

The tape diagram breaks $7\times8$  into $\purpleE{5\times8}$ and $\maroonE{2\times8}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
{7} \times {8} &=(\purpleE{5\times8}) + (\maroonE{2\times8})\\\\
&=\purpleE{40}+\maroonE{16}\\\\
&=56
\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the equations.**

||
-: | :-
$9 \times 4 $|$= \purpleE{10\text{ fours}}-{}$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\maroonE{\text{ four}}$|
|$= \purpleE{40} -\maroonE{4}$|
|$= $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]|
hints: Let's use an array to break the $9\times4$ into two parts.

The array breaks $9\text{ fours}$  into $\purpleE{10\text{ fours}}$ and $\maroonE{1\text{ four}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
{9} \times {4} &= \purpleE{10\text{ fours}}-\maroonE{1\text{ four}}\\\\
&=\purpleE{40}-\maroonE4\\\\
&=36
\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the equations.**

||
-: | :-
$9\times 6 $|$=$  $\purpleE{5\text{ sixes}}~ + \maroonE{4\text{ sixes}}$|
|$= \purpleE{30}~ +~$ [[☃ numeric-input 5]] |
|$=$ [[☃ numeric-input 6]]|
hints: Let's use a tape diagram to break the $9\times6$ into two parts.

The tape diagram breaks $9\text{ sixes}$  into $\purpleE{5\text{ sixes}}$ and $\maroonE{4\text{ sixes}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
9\times 6 =& ~\purpleE{5\text{ sixes}}~ + \maroonE {4{\text{ sixes}}}\\\\
\phantom{9\times 6 }=& ~\purpleE{30}{}+\maroonE{24}\\\\
\phantom{9\times 6 }=&~{} 54
\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the equations.**

||
-: | :-
$7\times 6 $|$= \purpleE{5\text{ sixes}} +\maroonE{2\text{ sixes}}$|
|$=$ [[☃ numeric-input 5]] ${}+\maroonE{12}$|
|$=$ [[☃ numeric-input 6]]|
hints: Let's use a tape diagram to break the $7\times6$ into two parts.

The tape diagram breaks $7\text { sixes}$  into $\purpleE{5\text{ sixes}}$ and $\maroonE{2\text{ sixes}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
7\times 6 =& ~\purpleE{5\text{ sixes}}~ + \maroonE {2{\text{ sixes}}}\\\\
\phantom{7\times 6 }=& ~\purpleE{30}{}+\maroonE{12}\\\\
\phantom{7\times 6 }=&~{} 42
\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the equations.**

||
-: | :-
$8\times 4 $|$= \purpleE{4\text{ fours}} +\maroonE{4\text{ fours}}$|
|$=$ [[☃ numeric-input 5]] ${}+\maroonE{16}$|
|$=$ [[☃ numeric-input 6]]|
hints: Let's use a tape diagram to break the $8\times4$ into two parts.

The tape diagram breaks $8\text{ fours}$  into $\purpleE{4\text{ fours}}$ and $\maroonE{4\text{ fours}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
8\times 4 =& ~\purpleE{4\text{ fours}}~ + \maroonE {4{\text{ fours}}}\\\\
\phantom{8\times 4 }=& ~\purpleE{16}{}+\maroonE{16}\\\\
\phantom{8\times 4 }=&~{} 32
\end{align}$
Let's use a tape diagram to break the $7\times4$ into two parts.

The tape diagram breaks $7\text{ fours}$  into $\purpleE{5\text{ fours}}$ and $\maroonE{2\text{ fours}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
7\times 4 =& ~\purpleE{5\text{ fours}}~ + \maroonE {2{\text{ fours}}}\\\\
\phantom{7\times 4 }=& ~\purpleE{20}{}+\maroonE{8}\\\\
\phantom{7\times 4 }=&~{} 28
\end{align}$
Let's use a tape diagram to break the $8\times8$ into two parts.

The tape diagram breaks $8\text{ eights}$  into $\purpleE{5\text{ eights}}$ and $\maroonE{3\text{ eights}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
8\times 8 =& ~\purpleE{5\text{ eights}}~ + \maroonE {3{\text{ eights}}}\\\\
\phantom{8\times 8 }=& ~\purpleE{40}{}+\maroonE{24}\\\\
\phantom{8\times 8 }=&~{} 64
\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the equations.**

||
-: | :-
$9\times9$|$= \purpleE{10\text{ nines}}- \maroonE{1\text{ nine}}$|
|$=\purpleE{90} - {}$[[☃ numeric-input 5]] |
|$=$ [[☃ numeric-input 6]]|
hints: Let's use an array to break the $9\times9$ into two parts.

The array breaks $10\text{ nines}$  into $\purpleE{9\text{ nines}}$ and $\maroonE{1\text{ nine}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 2]]

$\begin{align}
9\times 9 =& ~\purpleE{10\text{ nines}}~ - \maroonE {1{\text{ nine}}}\\\\
\phantom{9\times 9 }=& ~\purpleE{90}{}-\maroonE{9}\\\\
\phantom{9\times 9 }=&~{} 81
\end{align}$
